subject: Sociology
title: Media Ownership and Concentration
description: A quick, practical guide to Media Ownership and Concentration.

hook.text: Six corporations control 90% of American media. Six boardrooms shape what 330 million people see and hear.

buildup.text: Media concentration means fewer companies own more outlets: newspapers, TV networks, streaming platforms, and studios.

discovery.text: Owners don't need to censor directly. They set agendas, hire editors, and define what's newsworthy.

twist.text: Social media promised democratization. Instead, Facebook, Google, and Amazon became new media monopolies.

climax.text: When a few corporations control information, democracy's marketplace of ideas narrows to a handful of sellers.

punchline.text: Free press needs more than one owner.

quiz.question: Why does media ownership concentration concern sociologists?
options: A few corporations control what millions see and hear
